title: Write assertions that capture design rules
description: Express protocol, timing, and invariant rules as assertions so violations are caught the moment they occur.
body: Use when a design has rules that must always hold, such as a handshake never dropping mid-transaction. Do not bury rule checks in scattered procedural code, since assertions document intent and fire precisely at violation. Actions: identify the invariants, write each as a concurrent assertion, and verify the assertion fires on a deliberately injected violation. Validation signals include each assertion catching its injected fault and no spurious firings under legal stimulus. Risk boundary: an assertion that never fires may be vacuously true, so escalate to a fault-injection check confirming each assertion can actually trigger.
